subject: Automation adds oom_project_description.rst
commit_message:
Automation adds oom_project_description.rst

Add additional labels to create consistency across
all docs.

Issue-ID: CIMAN-376
Signed-off-by: Jessica Wagantall <jwagantall@linuxfoundation.org>
Change-Id: I6d3dd67adb6669fb897e4978cb88e3b7404d2897

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 2 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/4477/ (1/3)

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/doc-master-verify-rtd/6864/ (2/3)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

When I asked on https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/105429 and on https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/105390 to have a meaningful commit message, what wasn’t clear?

I did the job on the two previous one, I will not on this one. 

I’m saying it again: « So, add an explanation in the commit message in why we do this commit (like for every commit we do) »

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange 3: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

Oops, I’m may have overreacted a little bit (just saw the title on the email). 

The commit message has a sentence explaining the stuff. So it’s good for me. 

Sorry Jessica for that!
Waiting for job builder for merge

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
> 
> Oops, I’m may have overreacted a little bit (just saw the title on the email). 
> 
> The commit message has a sentence explaining the stuff. So it’s good for me. 
> 
> Sorry Jessica for that!
> Waiting for job builder for merge

Of course « a little bit » is ironic ;-)
